steps.title: Identify the conditional probability setup
steps.content: The phrase given that the tree is a red maple signals conditional probability: we need $P(\text{Site A} | \text{Red Maple})$. This means: among all red maples in the table, what fraction come from Site A? We restrict our sample space to the red maple row only.

steps.title: Extract table values and calculate
steps.content:
From the red maple row:
  • Red maples from Site A: 35
  • Total red maples: 50
The probability is $$\frac{35}{50} = \frac{7}{10} = 0.7$$
